title: Streaming Sitemap XML, Index Graphs, and Admission Bounds · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs streaming sitemap xml, index graphs, and admission bounds from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for streaming bounded sitemap graph after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Use encoding/xml tokens to validate namespace, root, UTF-8, loc, escaping, depth, counts, bytes, and trailing content without whole-tree allocation.
Model sitemap indexes as a bounded directed graph with unique identities, depth limits, cycle detection, and stable traversal.
Apply protocol location rules plus authorization, URL, robots, redirect, DNS, and SSRF policy to every sitemap and page entry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Sitemap Provenance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Go team handles case 5926 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to stream bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance admission; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Final URL alone explains sitemap discovery.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause. Artifact under review: streaming bounded sitemap graph.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Sitemap Provenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Sitemap Provenance
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Sitemap Provenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 7545 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to stream bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance admission; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Sitemap Provenance.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Sitemap Provenance, keep Sitemap Provenance connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Sitemap Token Stream.
